title: I Don'T Like It
lyrics:
[opening]
*Clink!* goes the dinosaur plate—
I don’t wanna eat it, wait!
Yesterday I liked it fine,
today it’s yucky—*splat!*—in a line!

[verse 1]
I don’t like the shape today,
it looks like a lizard, not okay!
The ketchup’s too red, it’s scary,
and the plate’s too blue—it’s hairy!

[chorus]
I don’t like it! No no no!
You can’t make me, here we go!
Mama says, “Just one small bite?”
I say, “NO!” and hide it—*crash!*—outta sight!

[verse 2]
What if it turns into a T-Rex?
It’ll chase me right out the door!
What if it grows a thousand legs?
I’ll scream and scream forevermore!

[chorus]
I don’t like it! No no no!
You can’t make me, here we go!
Mama says, “Just one small bite?”
I say, “NO!” and hide it—*crash!*—outta sight!

[verse 3]
I’ll feed it to the moon instead,
it’ll bounce up there and turn to bread!
Or I’ll launch it on a rocket,
it’ll land in space—*zoom!*—now it’s pocket!

[chorus]
I don’t like it… mmm… okay…
Maybe just a tiny taste…
*Yawn*… the plate’s so sleepy too…
I’ll eat it… zzz… goodnight to you…

[Cover: A tiny kid in pajamas launching a dinosaur nugget like a rocket, ketchup flames behind, parents facepalming in slow-motion shock.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, banjo and shaker, 117 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The kitchen clock reads 5:47 PM, and you just slid the blue plastic plate with the dinosaur-shaped chicken nuggets toward her high chair. She shoves it back, lips pursed, and yells, "I don't like it!"—even though she ate them yesterday.
cover_description: a cartoon child with funny expression in a cozy bedroom
